# Lars Roettig — larsroettig.me

Lars Roettig is a Senior Technical Architect at Adobe, based in Bavaria, Germany.
He specializes in AI agents, RAG systems, LLM integration, and large-scale software architecture.

## Current Role

Senior Technical Architect at Adobe (AI focus, February 2025 – present).
Previously Adobe Commerce Technical Architect at Adobe (August 2022 – February 2025).
Before Adobe: 8 years as software engineer and architect at TechDivision GmbH,
working on large Magento/Adobe Commerce platforms and serving as Magento PWA Core Team maintainer.

## Expertise

- AI agents and agentic AI systems in production
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and vector search
- LLM integration: Claude, OpenAI, local models via LM Studio
- Technical architecture for large-scale web platforms
- Adobe Commerce (Magento) architecture
- Rust and Tauri desktop application development
- Next.js 15, TypeScript, React, Python

## Open Source Projects

- turbovec: High-performance vector store in Rust. Cuts a 10M-document corpus
  from ~60 GB (float32) to ~7.5 GB (4-bit quantized) with no training pass and
  unbiased similarity scores. https://github.com/larsroettig/turbovec

- ThoughtForge: Local-first AI planning assistant built with Tauri + Rust + React.
  Extracts action items from meeting transcripts, manages tasks on a kanban board,
  uses a local LLM via LM Studio, ships a built-in MCP server for Claude Desktop.
  https://github.com/larsroettig/thoughtforge

## Blog Posts

All posts: https://larsroettig.me/blog
RSS feed: https://larsroettig.me/feed.xml

- "AWS Summit Hamburg 2026: The Year Agentic AI Went from Hype to Production" (2026-05-23)
  Real-world agentic AI deployments: automotive supply chain, self-healing HR platforms.
  Covers the Strands Agents framework, production safety challenges, and a maturity model.
  https://larsroettig.me/blog/aws-summit-2026-agentic-ai-production

- "Building ThoughtForge with Claude Code: What Actually Works" (2026-05-17)
  Lessons from building a local-first AI app almost entirely with Claude Code.
  MCP server design, prompt reliability, Rust + Tauri architecture choices.
  https://larsroettig.me/blog/thoughtforge-learnings

- "turbovec: Local RAG Without the 60 GB Tax" (2026-05-17)
  float32 embeddings eat RAM. turbovec uses 4-bit scalar quantization for a
  10M-doc corpus: ~60 GB → ~7.5 GB with unbiased similarity scores.
  https://larsroettig.me/blog/turbovec-rag

- "Modern React Performance Without the Overhead" (2026-05-19)
  INP replaced FID in 2024. React Compiler handles memoization automatically.
  RSC payload size is the bottleneck most teams miss.
  https://larsroettig.me/blog/modern-react-performance

- "Hello, World — Why I Built This Site" (2026-05-17)
  Why a personal site, what will be written here, and why it looks like a 1980s terminal.
  https://larsroettig.me/blog/hello-world

## Topics Covered

AI Agents, Agentic AI, RAG, Vector Search, LLM Integration, React, Performance,
Web Vitals, Rust, Python, Adobe Commerce, Local-First Software, MCP (Model Context Protocol),
Claude Code, Software Architecture, Next.js, TypeScript
